Title: MSD1 regulates pedicellate spikelet fertility in sorghum through the jasmonic acid pathway

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive; used for transient MSD1-GFP localization in tobacco)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: BTx623; msd1 introgressed/tested in RTx437 background
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MSD1 is a high-value regulatory target for increasing grain number per panicle by releasing pedicellate spikelet fertility. Loss-of-function editing or recessive alleles could be deployed to increase seed set and panicle size, but dosage/tissue-timing control is important because excessive suppression of JA can alter branching and developmental balance; coupling with yield/stress-resilience evaluation is recommended.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MSD1 function lowers jasmonic acid accumulation in young panicles, abolishes normal pedicellate spikelet abortion, and converts pedicellate spikelets into fertile grain-producing flowers, thereby increasing grain number per panicle.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bulk segregant analysis with whole-genome sequencing; allelic series sequencing; co-segregation in F2; qRT-PCR and in situ hybridization; transient subcellular localization; RNA-seq; JA quantification; exogenous methyl-JA rescue of msd1 phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: MSD1 is the decisive developmental regulator that transiently activates JA biosynthetic genes in pedicellate spikelets; its loss reduces JA by >50% and this metabolic defect is sufficient to explain the mutant phenotype because exogenous methyl-JA rescues it.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse/field panicle development stages; exogenous methyl jasmonate treatment beginning at stage 3 or earlier rescues msd1; treatment after stage 4 does not rescue; prolonged >1 mM Me-JA reduces panicle size and branching.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enriched during inflorescence development, especially pedicellate spikelets at stage 4; detected in spikelet/floral meristems from stage 2 to stage 3 and strongly in anthers at stage 4.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species; enriched in developing inflorescence, especially stage 4 pedicellate spikelets.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Five putative JA biosynthetic target genes identified by RNA-seq were inferred as downstream targets, but individual direct physical interactors were not experimentally validated; JA biosynthetic pathway genes (unnamed individually in provided text)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonic acid biosynthesis and signaling controlling floral organ arrest/abortion in pedicellate spikelets.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing / bulk segregant sequencing; RNA-seq; NCBI SRA: SRP127741
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MSD1 is a monocot-specific class II TCP in the CYC/TB1 subgroup and is not the sorghum ortholog of maize TB1. Transcriptome evidence indicates JA biosynthetic genes, rather than broader JA signaling genes, are especially reduced in msd1 pedicellate spikelets at stage 4.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA is associated with altered panicle branching/size; exogenous JA above optimal levels severely retards growth and produces smaller panicles, implying that manipulating MSD1/JA may have developmental tradeoffs and possibly stress-related costs due to JA pathway perturbation.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Jasmonic acid biosynthetic genes/enzymes identified by expression pattern analysis; sorghum orthologs of Arabidopsis JA biosynthesis pathway genes (five putative targets in Fig. 4a, not individually named in provided text)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II TCP transcription factor; CYC/TB1 subgroup; TCP DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of JA biosynthetic pathway genes in pedicellate spikelets
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of JA biosynthetic genes in pedicellate spikelets, especially around stage 4; their expression is reduced in msd1 mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSD1 was identified as Sb07g021140/SbTCP16 by bulk segregant sequencing and mutant allelic analysis. 2. MSD1 is highly expressed in developing inflorescences, especially stage-4 pedicellate spikelets, and encodes a nuclear TCP transcription factor. 3. MSD1 activates jasmonic acid biosynthetic genes, raising JA levels in young panicles. 4. Loss of MSD1 reduces JA by more than 50%, prevents pedicellate spikelet abortion, and increases grain number per panicle. 5. Exogenous methyl-JA rescues the msd1 phenotype, functionally linking MSD1 to JA-mediated floral repression.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing young panicles, particularly pedicellate spikelets during inflorescence development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Oxylipin jasmonate hormone derived from fatty acid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls floral organ arrest/abortion in pedicellate spikelets and influences inflorescence architecture; more broadly involved in plant development and stress responses.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: A developmental signal during stage 3 was proposed to activate MSD1 expression in pedicellate spikelets; no direct upstream regulator was experimentally validated in this paper.
